meta.title: About
meta.description: About Matteo Vittori, a Computer Science student focused on modular architecture, computer vision, and web products.

hero.description: I am a Computer Science student at the University of Camerino, graduating in 2026. I focus on modular architecture, computer vision, and full-stack product development.

approach.title: I care about how software changes.
paragraphs: My strongest projects begin with a structural question: what should remain stable while algorithms, libraries, interfaces, or requirements evolve?
That question shaped SEF’s plugin architecture, UniStays’ room-level product model, and the use-case boundaries in the Hackathon Management System.

items.title: Boundaries before frameworks
items.description: I begin by identifying responsibilities, invariants, and change points. Tools belong at the edge of that model.

items.title: Evidence over adjectives
items.description: Benchmarks, diagrams, tests, and working flows communicate more than claims about quality or scale.

items.title: Interfaces reveal systems
items.description: A good interface helps users understand state and consequences instead of masking the underlying model.
